category_title: Students using inappropriate language and avoiding assignment tasks in computer labs
minimal_context: The student is seated in the computer laboratory to research their topic and draft text for a persuasive marketing brochure using a scaffolding template.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please open your scaffolding template on the computer and start researching and drafting the text for your marketing brochure.

STUDENT: This is stupid. I find this curriculum completely irrelevant and it's too hard.

TUTOR: Just look at the models provided and try to write something down.

STUDENT: I don't even want to be here. School is just a shelter from unemployment anyway.

TUTOR: You need to complete the tasks to pass the subject. Please keep working.

STUDENT: (Stands up and leaves the desk) This is fucking bullshit. I'm not doing any more work, that's all I'm doing for the whole 70 minutes.

options.A: Have the student start out in a team of two to draft each section of the brochure.
options.B: Tell the student, 'Don't say you can't, you can,' to encourage them to continue the task.
options.C: Use humor to moderate the student's disruptive behavior (e.g., 'I see someone had sugar at lunch').
options.D: Provide the student with freedom and autonomy in the research and drafting activities for the brochure.
